faqs.q: What does body dysmorphic disorder mean?
faqs.a: It's a mental health condition in which a person becomes intensely preoccupied with one or more perceived flaws in their appearance that others barely notice or don't see at all. The preoccupation can take up hours a day and get in the way of work, school, and relationships.

faqs.q: Is body dysmorphic disorder the same as vanity?
faqs.a: No. It isn't vanity or being overly concerned with looks. It's a distressing, time-consuming condition the person doesn't want and can't simply set aside, and clinicians group it with obsessive-compulsive and related conditions.

faqs.q: How is body dysmorphic disorder treated?
faqs.a: It responds well to specific talk therapy and, for many people, medication. If worries about appearance are taking over hours of the day, an evaluation is worth it. If thoughts of self-harm come up, reach out to a professional right away. In the US you can call or text 988 any time.
